name: saena.tenant_id
description: Tenant identifier attribute, mirrors envelope tenant_id (ADR-0013). pii=false (not a name/email/etc.) but IS a re-identifying / identifying value under the 3-context model — ADR-0006 rev.2 requires its removal (not mere optionality) from AggregateContext to prevent re-identification, and it is exempted (forbidden) from SystemContext per ADR-0013. Treat as identifying for redaction/export-policy purposes even though the pii flag on this entry is false.

name: saena.run_id
description: Run identifier attribute, mirrors envelope run_id (ADR-0013). Marked required for TenantContext because W0 registry scope covers run-scoped telemetry; ADR-0013 leaves the nullable allowance for non-run TenantContext events OPEN (user decision pending, see ADR-0013 "Open decisions") — this registry entry does not resolve that OPEN item and must be revisited once ADR-0013 is amended or a follow-up ADR lands. Forbidden in SystemContext (property-level exemption) and AggregateContext (property-level removal, re-identification prevention), per ADR-0013's context_type table.

name: saena.engine_id
description: Search engine identifier, closed vocabulary aligned to envelope payload engine_id (ADR-0013): v1 closed enum ["chatgpt-search"] only — CLAUDE.md Engine scope v1 (Google AI Overviews/AI Mode/Gemini disabled for optimize/observe/claim). Optional at the general attribute level in all three contexts, but REQUIRED for the observation/citation/experiment event families specifically (ADR-0013 payload requirement) — that per-family requirement is stricter than this general per-context default and must be enforced at the event/payload contract level (packages/contracts, W1), not solely via this registry entry.

name: saena.context
description: Attribute-level mirror of the envelope context_type discriminator (ADR-0013 enum: tenant|system|aggregate). Same vocabulary as context_type — no separate vocabulary is created (ADR-0016 constraint). Low cardinality (3 values) — safe for span/metric naming inputs if ever needed, though ADR-0016 keeps identifiers out of names regardless. Required in all three contexts: every span/log/metric must declare which context it was emitted under.

name: saena.patch_unit_id
description: Identifies the implementation patch unit (worktree/unit-of-work) that produced the telemetry, e.g. "w1-04-quality-adrs" (see ADR-0013 example instance, patch.unit.completed.v1 payload). Operationally useful in TenantContext (workflow lead-time dashboards, observability.md dashboard themes) and SystemContext (adapter/config change attribution). Forbidden in AggregateContext: a patch unit ID is a high-cardinality, potentially re-identifying operational detail with no legitimate role in de-identified aggregate telemetry.

name: saena.contract_hash
description: Hash of the applicable contract/schema version in force at emission time (contracts SSOT per ADR-0011, packages/contracts). Useful for correlating telemetry with a specific contract revision across all three contexts; not identifying to a natural person or tenant lineage, so optional (not forbidden) in AggregateContext.

name: saena.policy_bundle_hash
description: Hash of the policy bundle in force, mirrors the run trace envelope field policy_bundle_hash (observability.md CONFIRMED fields, k3s §9.1). Optional in TenantContext (and, by consistency with the low re-identification risk of a policy bundle version marker, optional rather than forbidden in System/AggregateContext) — a bundle hash identifies a policy version, not a tenant or individual.

name: saena.aggregate_scope_id
description: Mirrors the envelope AggregateContext required field aggregate_scope_id (ADR-0013) — identifies the de-identified cohort/aggregate scope, never an individual tenant or run. Required only in AggregateContext; forbidden in TenantContext and SystemContext since it has no meaning outside an aggregate/de-identified scope and its presence there would signal aggregate-context leakage into non-aggregate telemetry.

name: saena.demand_graph_version
description: Content-addressed `sha256:<64-hex>` version hash of a built demand graph (mirrors services/intelligence/demand-graph-service `DemandGraph.graph_version` / the `demand.graph.versioned.v1` event payload field of the same name, w4-02/w4-10). A hash, never the graph's own paraphrase text/provenance content — safe to carry across all three contexts as an opaque version marker, matching saena.contract_hash's optional-everywhere precedent.

name: saena.entity_graph_version
description: Content-addressed `sha256:<64-hex>` version hash of a resolved entity graph (mirrors services/intelligence/entity-resolution-service `EntityResolutionResult.graph_version` / the `entity.graph.versioned.v1` event payload field, w4-03/w4-10). Same opaque-hash discipline as saena.demand_graph_version — never the resolved entity names/aliases themselves.

name: saena.claim_id
description: Identifier of one extracted claim in the claim-evidence ledger (mirrors services/intelligence/claim-evidence-service `ExtractedClaim.claim_id` / `ClaimPublishability.claim_id`, w4-04). An opaque id, never the claim's own `claim_text` (which must never appear in telemetry at all — see redaction-rules.yaml). Forbidden in AggregateContext: a claim id is a high-cardinality, potentially re-identifying operational detail, same rationale as saena.patch_unit_id.

name: saena.evidence_id
description: Identifier of one evidence record linked to a claim (mirrors services/intelligence/claim-evidence-service `EvidenceRecord.evidence_id`, w4-04). An opaque id, never the evidence's own `source_uri`/`excerpt` (customer-source-bearing fields that must never appear in telemetry — see redaction-rules.yaml). Forbidden in AggregateContext, same rationale as saena.claim_id.

name: saena.claim_publishable
description: The boolean outcome of `evaluate_claim_publishability` (w4-04) — whether a claim currently has at least one present/fresh/unblocked evidence link. Two-valued (low cardinality), carries no claim/evidence content itself, so it is safe in all three contexts including de-identified aggregate rollups (e.g. "% of claims publishable" dashboards) — contrast saena.claim_id/saena.evidence_id, which stay forbidden in aggregate.

name: saena.citation_normalized_uri_hash
description: `sha256:<64-hex>` digest of a citation's normalized URI (mirrors services/intelligence/citation-intelligence-service `normalize_url` output, w4-05) — a hash of the normalized URI, NEVER the URI string itself (a raw/normalized URL is customer-source-adjacent, high-cardinality, and potentially re-identifying; this entry exists so citation-normalization telemetry can correlate/count without ever carrying the URL). Forbidden in AggregateContext, same rationale as saena.claim_id.

name: saena.browser_pool_state
description: Closed-vocabulary lifecycle state of one chatgpt-observer browser-pool session (mirrors services/acquisition/chatgpt-observer-service `BrowserPool` acquire/release/recycle/close lifecycle, w4-08): one of "acquired", "released", "recycled", "closed". Low cardinality, no session/query content — safe in all three contexts.

name: saena.experiment_id
description: Identifier of one pre-registered experiment (mirrors packages/domain/src/saena_domain/experiment `ExperimentRegistration.experiment_id`, w4-09). An opaque id, never the registration's own query_cluster_ref/asset_hash content beyond this identifier. Forbidden in AggregateContext, same rationale as saena.claim_id — this registry entry carries no outcome/lift/causal value (W4/Wave-5-forbidden scope, CLAUDE.md "Current decision"); it identifies a REGISTRATION event only.

name: saena.intent_label
description: Closed 9-value query-cluster intent taxonomy (mirrors services/intelligence/demand-graph-service `IntentLabel` enum, w4-02: "definition|integration|security|pricing|comparison|implementation|migration|support|procurement" — a fixed, low-cardinality label, never the underlying paraphrase/query text — safe in all three contexts, including de-identified aggregate rollups (e.g. "graph builds by intent" dashboards).
